jokes.text: Free dinner from St. Elmo Steak House bait and switch - Ron immediately reveals his identity when offered free steak
jokes.setup: Process server uses fake prize to lure Ron out
jokes.punchline: Ron's love of meat overrides caution - instantly blows his cover
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ron's carnivore obsession creates his own downfall. Excellent setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: I don't like you. But I respect the effort.
jokes.setup: Ron just got served lawsuit papers through deception
jokes.punchline: Ron appreciates the craftsmanship of his own deception
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron - respects competence even when it hurts him. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: $46 million for psychological damages
jokes.setup: Reasonable medical costs for dental work
jokes.punchline: Absurdly inflated psychological damage claim
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure but fairly standard frivolous lawsuit joke.

jokes.text: Yeah, that seems about accurate.
jokes.punchline: Tom treats it as reasonable assessment
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction beat - Tom's casual acceptance of absurd number works for his character.

jokes.text: We should sue Jamm's parents for spawning a human turdburger.
jokes.setup: Jamm is awful and suing Ron
jokes.punchline: Counter-sue parents for creating 'human turdburger'
jokes.explanation: 'Turdburger' is a great Tom-style insult - creative and juvenile simultaneously.

jokes.text: As I do in all legal matters and livestock auctions.
jokes.setup: Ron will represent himself in court
jokes.punchline: Casually equates legal matters with livestock auctions
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron character moment - reveals his rural background through unexpected comparison.

jokes.text: I wish we had $46 million. We could buy my parents' house and evict them.
jokes.punchline: Tom's first thought is evicting his own parents
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Tom's petty vindictiveness toward family.

jokes.text: Ron's delayed violent reaction after appearing calm, followed by the crash sound
jokes.setup: Ron appears to be taking the lawsuit well
jokes.punchline: Sudden outburst and destruction
jokes.explanation: Classic delayed reaction comedy. Ron's composure finally breaking is satisfying physical comedy.

jokes.text: And none of them had as cute a butt as you do.
jokes.setup: Leslie praising Ben compared to other mayors
jokes.punchline: Switches from professional to personal/physical compliment
jokes.explanation: Typical Leslie over-enthusiasm spilling into inappropriate territory.

jokes.text: You're so sexy when you talk about percentages.
jokes.punchline: Leslie finds statistical talk arousing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - her nerd enthusiasm becomes romantic attraction.

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't the defendant, Ron Swansuck.
jokes.punchline: Childish name-calling portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but fits Jamm's petty character. Low-effort wordplay.

jokes.text: This lawsuit is Chronicles of Riddick-ulous.
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference pun combining movie title with 'ridiculous'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom - unnecessarily convoluted pop culture reference when simple word would do.

jokes.text: The nurse still won't have sex with me, and she's not even queer.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate assumption about nurse's sexuality
jokes.explanation: Shows Jamm's gross entitlement and poor logic - character-appropriate cringe.

jokes.text: It's what we in the Jamm family call... 'Payback time.' / Everyone calls it that. / Yeah, because I invented it.
jokes.punchline: Tom points out it's universal, Jamm claims he invented common phrase
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jamm's delusional self-importance and Tom's deflation.

jokes.text: I'm not not going to win nothing.
jokes.punchline: Triple negative creating confusing grammar
jokes.explanation: Shows Jamm's poor communication skills through mangled grammar.

jokes.text: They once got him $60,000 'cause he got too scared at a haunted house.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous successful lawsuit over being scared at haunted house
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd backstory that feels real for this universe - great world-building joke.

jokes.text: Who were you competing against? / My own taste buds.
jokes.setup: Chris claims he won gardening contest
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was only competing against himself
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure revealing Chris's self-obsession in funny way.

jokes.text: You wanna make a baby, Traeger? Your hair, my everything else. That kid would be unstoppable.
jokes.setup: Tom and Chris score high on compatibility test
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately proposes genetic combination with Chris
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom - immediately competitive and vain about his genetic superiority.

jokes.text: Yes, we did not win.
jokes.setup: Ann and Chris fail the compatibility test
jokes.punchline: Chris's overly positive spin on failure
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris - maintaining positivity even while acknowledging failure.

jokes.text: Yeah, get used to it. We're sisters now.
jokes.punchline: Immediate declaration of sisterhood through marriage
jokes.explanation: Realistic family dynamic joke - immediate intimacy assumption.

jokes.text: Who were Ben's high school girlfriends, and what base do you think he got to?
jokes.setup: Leslie wants to bond with Ben's sister
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately intimate questions about Ben's sexual history
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie over-enthusiasm creating awkward sister-in-law moment.

jokes.text: Well, I pooched the brain exam. Just to be safe, I wouldn't take anything I say too seriously.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why he failed police application
jokes.punchline: Warns court not to trust his own testimony
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy moment - completely undermines his own testimony while trying to help.

jokes.text: Ronny? Are you kidding me? I've never heard him threaten to hurt anyone or anything. The guy's, like, a vegetarian.
jokes.punchline: Calls meat-obsessed Ron a vegetarian
jokes.explanation: Great lie that's completely opposite to Ron's character - audience knows it's absurd.

jokes.text: His nickname around the office is 'Softy-Pants McHuggable.'
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous nickname for stoic Ron
jokes.explanation: Hilarious fabricated nickname that's perfectly opposite to Ron's actual personality.

jokes.text: Also, may I say for the record, that is a dope pocket square. Can I see that bad boy?
jokes.punchline: Completely derails to compliment lawyer's fashion
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - inappropriate timing but genuine appreciation for style.

jokes.text: There's only one thing I hate more than lying. Skim milk. Which is water that's lying about being milk.
jokes.punchline: Connects it to his hatred of skim milk via metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted Ron philosophy - connects moral stance to food preferences with clever metaphor. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I think about where you've been and where you're going and our future children and America and just our history as a nation.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why Ben's speech made her cry
jokes.punchline: Escalates from personal to cosmic significance
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie escalation from personal moment to national significance.

jokes.text: I think I'm giving birth. Is that possible? No, right?
jokes.setup: Ben in severe pain from kidney stone
jokes.punchline: Confused about biological impossibility
jokes.explanation: Standard male childbirth comparison for kidney stone pain.

jokes.text: Oh, God! It's twins!
jokes.punchline: Makes it worse by claiming twins
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the birth metaphor but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Do you have any idea how beautiful you are? I'm serious. You're like a flower. Or, no, like a snug-bug or like a snugger-doodle or something like that.
jokes.setup: Ben on morphine trying to compliment Leslie
jokes.punchline: Devolves into nonsense made-up words
jokes.explanation: Good drug-induced rambling showing Ben's sweet but confused state.

jokes.text: Sorry, Ice Town, you're not going anywhere for at least a day.
jokes.setup: Doctor addressing Ben about staying in hospital
jokes.punchline: Calls him by his infamous nickname
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how Ben's past follows him everywhere, even medical settings.

jokes.text: I bought 12 babies, and then I immediately abandoned them at a Kroger's.
jokes.setup: Ann forgot eggs at grocery store
jokes.punchline: Describes forgetting eggs as abandoning babies
jokes.explanation: Great commitment to the egg-baby metaphor creating dark comedy about abandonment.

jokes.text: I was like Mark Z in the Social 'N.'
jokes.punchline: Abbreviates 'The Social Network' in pretentious way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom - trying to sound cool with unnecessary abbreviation.

jokes.text: You even called me a... [whispers] vegetarian.
jokes.punchline: Treats 'vegetarian' as unspeakable insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron reaction - treats vegetarian label as ultimate character assassination.

jokes.text: I only tell the truth when it makes me sound like I'm lying.
jokes.punchline: April's backwards logic about honesty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April paradox - perfectly captures her contrarian nature in philosophical terms.

jokes.text: It's how I was able to scrape by on the streets of Bombay and make it on the Indian version of Who Wants to Be a Millionaire and get reunited with the love of my life, Latika.
jokes.setup: Tom talking about lying being all he knows
jokes.punchline: Recites plot of Slumdog Millionaire as his life story
jokes.explanation: Great Tom moment - appropriating movie plot as personal history while claiming to tell truth.

jokes.text: Sometimes when I blow my nose, I get a boner. I don't know why. It just happens. Truth bomb.
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate overshare about bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - complete lack of social boundaries while earnestly trying to help.

jokes.text: I feel like I never gave Miles Davis proper shrift. Like, I just didn't get into it.
jokes.punchline: Random jazz appreciation epiphany while high
jokes.explanation: Good drug-induced non-sequitur showing Ben's earnest nerd nature even while high.

jokes.text: Fred the Sled? They only bring him out for special occasions.
jokes.punchline: Ben knows obscure mascot protocol while drugged
jokes.explanation: Shows Ben still remembers hometown details despite years away and drug haze.

jokes.text: He looks like he could use a swift punch in the face though.
jokes.setup: Ron claiming he's never met Jamm
jokes.punchline: Immediately wants to punch him based on appearance alone
jokes.explanation: Great Ron moment showing his instinctive dislike of Jamm-types even without history.

jokes.text: If it were me, I would have just punched him in the face.
jokes.setup: Tom describing pushing Jamm in pool
jokes.punchline: Ron immediately suggests violence as better solution
jokes.explanation: Shows Ron's direct approach to problem-solving through violence.

jokes.text: That's a typo. That's a major typo. That says 'Ice Clown.'
jokes.punchline: Banner says 'Ice Clown' instead of 'Ice Town'
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag making Ben's humiliation even worse with insulting 'typo'.

jokes.text: Uh-oh, the key was made of ice. And much like the hopes and dreams of the people of Partridge, it melted away.
jokes.punchline: Ice key melts while mayor makes cruel metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cruel visual gag with perfect metaphor connecting ice to broken dreams.

jokes.text: He has a calcified rock lodged in his penis!
jokes.punchline: Medically inaccurate but passionate defense
jokes.explanation: Leslie's passionate but anatomically confused defense of Ben's condition.

jokes.text: Well, BEN WYATT'S BETTER THAN LEE JANZEN, AND HE'S BETTER THAN JUDY GARLAND.
jokes.punchline: Leslie claims Ben is better than Judy Garland
jokes.explanation: Leslie's loyalty leading her to make absurd claims about Ben vs. iconic figures.

jokes.text: He says a lot of things. He's always talking about overthrowing the government, but he never does that.
jokes.setup: April defending Ron's threats as meaningless
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions his anti-government plotting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April logic - undermines her defense by revealing worse behavior as 'harmless'.

jokes.text: Truthfully, I barely registered his attack. He's incredibly frail, and his arms are weak. And when I punched him, he dropped so quickly, I thought he was diving towards the ground.
jokes.setup: Ron's court statement about the punch
jokes.punchline: Describes Jamm as pathetically weak with diving metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan delivery making Jamm sound pathetic while admitting guilt.

jokes.text: I regret nothing. The end.
jokes.punchline: Blunt refusal to show remorse with abrupt ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron conclusion - maximum defiance in minimum words. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You got jammed by your own team. Self-jamm!
jokes.setup: Ron's team hurt his case with truth
jokes.punchline: Jamm coins 'self-jamm' as new term
jokes.explanation: Jamm's continued evolution of his signature phrase - character-appropriate wordplay.

jokes.text: Really? / Six-time Tour de France champion.
jokes.punchline: Chris's absurdly specific athletic ambition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris - over-the-top athletic expectations showing his fitness obsession.

jokes.text: Are you guys complete strangers? Is this one of those Craigslist father situations?
jokes.setup: Ann and Chris show no compatibility
jokes.punchline: Doctor assumes worst-case scenario
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of fertility counselor's confusion about their relationship.

jokes.text: I do know how many pounds of money I have.
jokes.punchline: Measures money by weight, not value
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron financial philosophy - measuring wealth by physical weight reveals his anti-fiat currency stance.

jokes.text: I'm feeding your eagle. He's starving. / It's--it's bronze, babe.
jokes.punchline: Leslie has to explain it's not alive
jokes.explanation: Good drug-addled Ben confusion about reality - sweet attempt to help 'starving' bronze eagle.

jokes.text: Will you love our child and work hard to see that he or she has a good life? / You see, I think that's the only question that matters.
jokes.punchline: Simple question cutting through all complexity
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Chris's emotional intelligence beneath the fitness obsession.

jokes.text: You know there's nothing in there yet, right? / Yes, sorry.
jokes.punchline: Has to be reminded she's not actually pregnant yet
jokes.explanation: Standard pregnancy comedy but fits Chris's over-enthusiasm.

jokes.text: My face! Is my face still there? What happened to my knees?
jokes.setup: Tom faking injury from Jamm barely touching him
jokes.punchline: Absurdly dramatic overreaction to minor contact
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tom's fake injury performance - appropriately over-the-top.

jokes.text: One time, our boat almost tipped over, but then it didn't. / That's not a great story.
jokes.punchline: Admits his own story is boring
jokes.explanation: Sweet Ben moment showing his self-awareness about his mundane childhood.

jokes.text: I stole it from Mayor Stice's office. Screw that guy. You should have it.
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to theft for Ben's sake
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - casual criminality motivated by fierce loyalty to Ben.

jokes.text: Hey, lake's not a garbage can, pal. / Yeah, sorry, it was symbolic.
jokes.setup: Ben throws key in lake, local objects
jokes.punchline: Ben apologizes but defends symbolic gesture
jokes.explanation: Good Ben moment - polite even while being environmentally irresponsible.

jokes.text: Is that Ben Wyatt? Get the hell out of here, Ice Town!
jokes.setup: Ben and Leslie trying to leave quietly
jokes.punchline: Ben immediately recognized and harassed with nickname
jokes.explanation: Good final callback showing Ben can never escape his past, even in nature.
